data.title: Johnson & Johnson stock rises 23% YTD, Jim Cramer predicts it could surpass $200 amidst legal battles, change in strategy.
data.description: Johnson & Johnson (NYSE: JNJ) stock has increased by over 23% this year despite facing legal challenges regarding its talc products. CNBC's Jim Cramer attributes this growth to investor confidence in the company's pharmaceutical sector and a shift in its legal strategy. Johnson & Johnson has decided to contest lawsuits in court rather than opt for broad bankruptcy settlements, a move that is reshaping market perceptions. The stock had previously suffered due to legal issues, but the recent change in approach has garnered positivity from Wall Street. Additionally, Johnson & Johnson's subsidiary Red River Talc's request for a prepackaged bankruptcy plan was declined by a U.S. bankruptcy court in Texas, which included one of the largest settlements in mass tort bankruptcy history. This decision indicates a shift in how the company is handling its legal affairs.

data.title: ONDO price rises by 7.83% to $1.09 due to tokenized assets platform growth, hitting TVL milestones and expanding assets.
data.description: The cryptocurrency ONDO is currently trading at $1.09, showing a daily increase of 7.83%. This surge in value is a result of the platform reaching Total Value Locked (TVL) milestones and expanding its tokenized assets. The bullish technical breakout has attracted investors, leading to the price rise.

data.title: Health Tech Without Borders (HTWB) releases report from Global Health Survey Series, capturing data from over 1,000 clinicians amid 2025 cross-sectional survey.
data.description: In Boston, Health Tech Without Borders (HTWB), Inc., a global non-profit focused on enhancing access to digital health resources, has launched the initial report of its Global Health Survey Series. The survey, held in 2025, involved over 1,000 clinicians globally. The research was conducted by M3 Global Research and sponsored by Digital Health New York and the City University of New York School of Public Health Foundation. The survey aimed to record the utilization and contentment of healthcare professionals with evidence-based technologies.

data.title: Dow Jones Futures Fall, Google and Apple rise after antitrust ruling. Markets closed off lows.
data.description: After the major indices closed near their lows on Tuesday, Google surged and Apple rose late following a federal judge's antitrust ruling. Dow Jones Futures fell in the post-market session, but Google and Apple experienced notable increases due to the ruling.

data.title: Nintendo Direct announces new games for both Nintendo Switch 2 and Nintendo Switch, celebrating the 40th anniversary of Super Mario Bros.
data.description: In a recent Nintendo Direct event, a diverse range of games from Nintendo and its partners were highlighted. The showcased titles are set to release on either Nintendo Switch 2 or the original Nintendo Switch, with many games launching on both platforms. The games are slated to debut from now until 2025 and beyond. To mark the 40th anniversary of Super Mario Bros., Shigeru Miyamoto, the creator of Mario, initiated the presentation with various planned activities for the milestone celebration.

data.description: The Ethereum Name Service (ENS) is currently trading at $24.33, indicating promising signs for further price increase. The Relative Strength Index (RSI) for ENS stands at a neutral level of 52, while the Moving Average Convergence Divergence (MACD) is displaying bullish divergence. These technical indicators imply a potential upward trend in the near future for ENS.

data.title: Dow falls after weak jobs report; Nvidia drops, Tesla rises. Nasdaq gains for the week.
data.description: On Friday, the Dow Jones index experienced a decline following a disappointing August jobs report. Nvidia's stock saw a sell-off, whereas Tesla's stock showed a significant rise. The stock market today saw the Dow sliding while the Nasdaq managed to achieve weekly gains. Notably, Nvidia broke through a key level in its stock price. Tesla's stock, on the other hand, demonstrated a strong upward movement.

data.title: Syndax Pharmaceuticals granted inducement awards for 167,400 shares to 11 new employees, options will vest over 4 years.
data.description: On September 1, 2025, Syndax Pharmaceuticals (Nasdaq: SNDX) revealed that they awarded 167,400 shares of common stock to 11 new employees under their 2023 Inducement Plan. These stock options will vest over four years, with 25% of the shares vesting after one year from the commencement date and the rest vesting monthly over 36 months. This is subject to the employees remaining with Syndax during the vesting period.

data.title: Trump fired the BLS commissioner after a weaker-than-expected jobs report, sparking fallout in government data credibility.
data.description: President Trump recently took decisive action in response to a disappointing jobs report by dismissing the commissioner of the Bureau of Labor Statistics. This move reflects his longstanding doubts about the accuracy of government economic data. The report in question revealed a lower employment growth than anticipated, prompting the president to take action. This development highlights the tension surrounding economic data, with the government reporting a figure below expectations at that time.

data.title: July layoffs increased by 140% compared to last year. Fed inflation rate overshoots. S&P 500 sees gains.
data.description: In July, job cuts surged by 140% compared to the previous year. The Federal Reserve's preferred inflation measure exceeded expectations. Additionally, the S&P 500 index saw gains following the news.

data.title: DIAGNOS closes private placement with $100,000 raised from 10 units priced at $10,000 each. AI-based health tech company.
data.description: An AI technology company, Diagnos Inc., located in BROSSARD, Quebec, shared that it successfully completed a non-brokered private placement on September 5, 2025. The private placement involved issuing 10 units at $10,000 per unit, resulting in total gross proceeds of $100,000. Diagnos Inc. is recognized for its expertise in early health issue detection and utilizes advanced AI technology for this purpose. The company is traded on TSX Venture under the symbol ADK, OTCQB under DGNOF, and on FWB as 4D4A.

data.title: Dow Jones index dips after data release, then recovers. Alphabet and Apple surge on court ruling.
data.description: On Wednesday, the Dow Jones index experienced a decline following the publication of important data. Alphabet and Apple shares saw a notable increase due to a court decision. The stock market today witnessed the Dow nearly completely recovering its losses after Federal Reserve data was released. Additionally, Warren Buffett's company's stock reversed its downward trend and rose.
